q.en: In 'The Fox and the Stork,' how did the stork get back at the fox for the soup trick?
q.zh: 在《狐狸和鹳》中，鹳是如何报复狐狸的汤之计的？

options.en: He hid the fox's food
options.zh: 他把狐狸的食物藏起来

options.en: He poured soup into a flat dish according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他把汤倒进浅盘里（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: He invited the fox to a movie
He served meat in a jar with a long neck that the fox could not reach
options.zh: 他把肉放在长颈罐里，狐狸够不到

explain.en: The stork 'placed some tasty meat in a jar with a long neck' so the fox 'could not' reach it, returning the favor for the soup trick.
explain.zh: 鹳'把美味的肉放在长颈罐里'，狐狸'够不到'，以此回敬了汤的捉弄。

q.en: What moral does 'The Fox and the Crow' teach?
q.zh: 《狐狸和乌鸦》告诉我们什么道理？

options.en: Birds should not sing
Always share your food
Do not trust strangers who try to flatter you
options.zh: 不要相信试图奉承你的陌生人

options.en: Slow and steady wins the race according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 稳扎稳打才能赢（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: After the fox flatters the crow and steals her cheese, the stated moral is 'Do not trust strangers who try to flatter you.'
explain.zh: 狐狸奉承乌鸦并偷走她的奶酪后，给出的寓意是'不要相信试图奉承你的陌生人'。

q.en: How did the crow in 'The Crow and the Pitcher' reach the water?
q.zh: 在《乌鸦和水罐》中，乌鸦是怎么喝到水的？

options.en: He waited for it to rain
He asked the fox for help
He dropped pebbles into the pitcher until the water rose
options.zh: 他把石子丢进水罐，直到水位上升

options.en: He tipped the whole pitcher over according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他把整个水罐打翻（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The text says the crow 'began dropping them [pebbles] into the pitcher. Slowly, the water began to rise' until he could drink.
explain.zh: 文中说乌鸦'把石子一颗颗丢进水罐。慢慢地，水位开始上升'，直到能喝到。

q.en: Why does the author put a moral at the end of each fable?
q.zh: 作者为什么在每个寓言结尾都写上寓意？

options.en: To describe the pictures
To list new vocabulary words according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了列出新词汇（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To make the book longer
To teach a lesson through the story
options.zh: 通过一个故事来传达道理

explain.en: The book's focus question is 'What can people learn from Aesop's fables?' Each fable ends with a moral so readers learn a lesson.
explain.zh: 书的焦点问题是'人们能从伊索寓言中学到什么？'每则寓言都以寓意结尾，让读者学到道理。

q.en: In 'The Peacock,' what advice does the goddess give the peacock?
q.zh: 在《孔雀》中，女神给了孔雀什么建议？

options.en: Treasure your own gifts because no one is good at everything
options.zh: 珍惜自己的天赋，因为没有人样样都行

options.en: Leave the forest forever
Steal the raven's food
Learn to sing like a nightingale according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 学着像夜莺一样唱歌（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The goddess says, 'No one is good at everything... You are special just as you are,' teaching the peacock to value his own gifts.
explain.zh: 女神说'没有人是样样都行的……你本来的样子就很特别'，教导孔雀珍惜自己的天赋。

q.en: In 'The City Mouse and the Country Mouse,' why did the country mouse go back home?
q.zh: 在《城市老鼠和乡下老鼠》中，乡下老鼠为什么回家了？

options.en: The city had no food
He preferred a simple, safe life to luxury and fear of the cat
options.zh: 他宁可过简单安全的生活，也不愿在奢华和怕猫中生活

options.en: He lost his way
He was invited to a party according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他受邀参加聚会（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The country mouse 'would rather live in the poor, quiet country than the crowded, dangerous city' with its pantry but also a hunting cat; moral: better to live simply in safety.
explain.zh: 乡下老鼠'宁愿住在贫穷安静的乡下，也不愿住拥挤危险的城里'——那里虽有食物却有捕鼠的猫；寓意：宁可在安全中简朴生活。

q.en: What lesson does 'The Wolf in Sheep's Clothing' teach?
q.zh: 《披着羊皮的狼》告诉我们什么道理？

options.en: The way things look is not always the way things are
options.zh: 事物的表象未必就是真相

options.en: Always trust strangers according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 总要相信陌生人（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Sheep should live alone
Wolves make good pets

explain.en: The stated moral is 'The way things look is not always the way things are.'
explain.zh: 给出的寓意是'事物的表象未必就是真相'。

q.en: Who retold the fables in this book?
q.zh: 这本书中的寓言由谁改写？

options.en: Julie Harding
Troy Wolff
Aesop himself
Maria Voris according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 玛丽亚·沃里斯（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The title page says 'Aesop's Fables ... Retold by Julie Harding.'
explain.zh: 书名页写着'《伊索寓言》……由朱莉·哈丁改写'。

q.en: What happens to the dog in 'The Dog and His Reflection' when he snaps at his reflection?
q.zh: 在《狗和它的倒影》中，狗朝自己的倒影扑去时发生了什么？

options.en: He makes a new friend according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他交了一个新朋友（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: He drops his own meat into the stream and loses it
options.zh: 他自己的肉掉进溪流里，丢了

options.en: He learns to swim
He finds more food

explain.en: The dog 'bent down and snapped at the other dog. As he did, his own meat fell from his mouth into the stream,' teaching that greed can cost what you have.
explain.zh: 狗'弯下腰朝另一只狗扑去。就在这时，他嘴里的肉掉进了溪流'，说明贪婪会让人失去已有的东西。

q.en: In 'The Fox and the Stork,' what did the fox first serve the stork to eat?
q.zh: 在《狐狸和鹳》中，狐狸起初给鹳端上什么吃？

options.en: Meat in a jar
Soup in a flat dish
Fish in a bowl
Bread on a plate according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 盘子里的面包（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The fox 'poured the soup into a flat dish' so the stork with his long beak 'could do nothing but dip the tip' and ate nothing.
explain.zh: 狐狸'把汤倒进浅盘里'，长嘴的鹳'只能蘸到一点点'，什么也没吃到。

prompt.en: Choose one fable from the book and write your own short version with a different animal, keeping the same moral.
prompt.zh: 从书中选一则寓言，用另一种动物写一个简短的新版本，保留同样的寓意。

en: Name the fable and its moral.
Change the animal characters but keep the lesson.
zh: 写出寓言名称及其寓意。
更换动物角色但保留道理。
